commit.message:
Fix LAN reconnect: add prefix matching for IPv6 hostname changes

Bonjour hostnames based on IPv6 SLAAC addresses change suffix when
machines move between networks. The exact deviceName match fails.
Add prefix-based matching (first 20 chars of hostname) as fallback
to resolve Bonjour peers to their Firestore worker UUID.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add Tailwind CSS for styling

- Add middleware for API protection
- Setup environment configuration

commit.message:
Upgrade LAN reconnect logs to notice level for visibility

info-level logs don't persist in macOS unified logging by default,
making LAN reconnect debugging impossible. Use notice level instead.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Revert aggressive candidate cleanup in sendOffer to reduce Firestore ops

The per-document candidate deletion was doing N+1 reads and deletes
per connection attempt. Combined with 15s retry loops across multiple
peers, this exhausted the Firestore free tier quota. Revert to just
deleting the stale remote answer doc (1 operation per attempt).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix sendable capture warning for effectivePeerId

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix LAN reconnect: resolve Bonjour hostname to Firestore UUID

When TXT record hasn't delivered the deviceId, look up the Bonjour
service name in the Firestore worker list to find the real UUID.
This fixes LAN connections being blocked when Bonjour TXT records
are delayed or missing.

Also fix sendable capture warning for effectivePeerId.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add AGENTS.md

fix: distinguish XtabProvider (Shown) from nes.nextCursorPosition (Accepted)

XtabProvider ccreq success lines represent NES fetching and displaying
a suggestion (Shown event), not user acceptance. Only
nes.nextCursorPosition — which fires after the user presses Tab — is
a true Accepted event.

Changes:
- Add recordInlineShown() helper in textLogParser.ts
- Route [XtabProvider] ccreq → recordInlineShown() (was recordInlineAccepted)
- Keep [nes.nextCursorPosition] ccreq → recordInlineAccepted()
- Update existing tests to expect correct Shown/Accepted classification
- Add new test suite verifying mixed real-log scenarios

Evidence from actual logs at ~/.vscode-server/data/logs/:
  ccreq:...|success|copilot-nes-oct|...|[XtabProvider]        → Shown
  ccreq:...|success|copilot-suggestions-himalia-001|...|[nes.nextCursorPosition] → Accepted

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Bonjour signaling mismatch and stale ICE candidate cleanup

- Skip LAN reconnect for peers whose TXT record hasn't delivered
  the real deviceId yet. Without the UUID, the Firestore signaling
  doc path uses the Bonjour service name, which the remote peer
  won't be listening for. Add hasDeviceId property to DiscoveredPeer.

- Clean up stale ICE candidates from both directions before sending
  a new SDP offer. Previously only the remote answer doc was deleted,
  leaving old candidates that would flood the receiveCandidates()
  listener on its first snapshot.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix os_log privacy redaction in PeerSession and SwarmCoordinator

Add privacy: .public to all diagnostic log interpolations so that
peer IDs, error messages, and connection state are visible in
'log show' output without requiring a profile override.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Feature/generation optimizations (#729)

* perf: optimize code generation pipeline

- Cache PropertyDeclarations ordering (avoids re-sorting on every access)
- Use StringComparer.Ordinal on 13 Dictionary<string,...> fields across
  JsonSchemaRegistry, JsonSchemaTypeBuilder, TypeDeclaration,
  PropertyDeclaration, VocabularyRegistry, DocumentResolvers, and
  CodeGenerator (avoids culture-aware comparison overhead on URIs/identifiers)
- Cache OrderedSubschemaTypeDeclarations on TypeDeclaration to avoid
  repeated LINQ OrderBy + ToString() allocations in recursive traversals
  (SetNames, MarkNonGeneratedTypes)
- Pre-compute indent strings for levels 0-19 in CodeGenerator, replacing
  a per-call loop with a single cached string Append

Combined with downstream Corvus.Text.Json.CodeGeneration optimizations,
measured speedup: SimpleObject 1.17x, ComplexObject 1.89x,
CompositionType 1.85x (BenchmarkDotNet, net10.0).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: add explicit 2/3/4-arg overloads and params ReadOnlySpan for Append methods

Add non-allocating overloads for AppendLineIndent, AppendIndent, and AppendLine:
- Explicit 2, 3, 4 Segment parameter overloads (all TFMs) to avoid params array allocation
- params ReadOnlySpan<Segment> on net9.0+ for 5+ args (stack-allocated via C# 13)
- params Segment[] retained on netstandard2.0/net8.0 for 5+ args

~460 multi-arg calls per type generation now avoid heap-allocating a Segment[].
Measured ~1-2% memory reduction and consistent throughput improvement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* perf: add explicit 2/3/4-arg overloads and params ReadOnlySpan for Append methods

Add non-allocating overloads for AppendLineIndent, AppendIndent, and AppendLine:
- Explicit 2, 3, 4 Segment parameter overloads (all TFMs) to avoid params array allocation
- params ReadOnlySpan<Segment> on net9.0+ for 5+ args (stack-allocated via C# 13)
- params Segment[] retained on netstandard2.0/net8.0 for 5+ args

~460 multi-arg calls per type generation now avoid heap-allocating a Segment[].
Measured ~1-2% memory reduction and consistent throughput improvement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: konarsubhojit <koner.subhojit@gmail.com>

fix(security): eliminate script injection vulnerabilities in action.yml (#85)

* Initial plan

* fix(security): prevent script injection in action.yml

- Pass `inputs.atmos-config-path` through env var and quote it in
  `realpath` call to prevent command injection
- Pass `inputs.base-ref` and `inputs.skip-checkout` through env vars
  and use a bash array in the `git checkout` call to prevent injection
- Remove the `affected-args` intermediate step that built a raw shell
  string from unquoted `${{ inputs.* }}` expressions
- Replace `eval` in both `atmos describe affected` invocations with
  bash arrays built from env vars, eliminating arbitrary code execution
  via user-controlled inputs (`atmos-stack`, `identity`, etc.)

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(security): DRY up repeated args-building logic between affected steps

Extract shared argument construction into a dedicated 'Build atmos
affected command args' step that writes args (one per line) to
${RUNNER_TEMP}/atmos-affected-args. Both 'affected-pro' and 'affected'
steps then read them back with mapfile, passing only the step-specific
flag (--upload or --file=) inline. No eval, no injection surface.

Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: nitrocode <7775707+nitrocode@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Andriy Knysh <aknysh@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add rebuild-and-continue pipeline for self-build loop

Core infrastructure enabling Peel to rebuild itself mid-chain:

- Add ChainCheckpointService.swift: serialize/restore chain state to
  ~/Library/Application Support/Peel/ChainCheckpoints/ as JSON
- Add RebuildToolsHandler.swift with 3 MCP tools:
  - chain.rebuild: runs Tools/build.sh and reports success/failure
  - chain.checkpoint: saves active chain state for later resume
  - chain.resume: lists or loads checkpointed chains
- Add StepType.checkpoint: new chain step type that saves state to disk
  and can trigger rebuild before continuing
- Add runCheckpointStep() in AgentChainRunner for checkpoint execution
- Add --resume-chain ID flag to build-and-launch.sh: after relaunch,
  loads checkpoint and prints resume instructions
- Wire RebuildToolsHandler into MCPServerService (property, init,
  delegate, tool definitions, dispatch)

This closes the self-build loop: agents can now checkpoint state →
rebuild the app → relaunch → resume where they left off.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Adds scripts/session-report.js — captures autonomous session activity via gh CLI and produces structured Markdown reports with YAML frontmatter. 33 tests with DI-mocked deps. Closes #48

commit.message:
Initial commit: Dimensional Breach movement prototype

Custom kinematic FPS controller built from scratch (no UFPS).
Unity 6000.3.11f1 / URP.

Movement systems implemented:
- Walk, sprint, air control, coyote time, jump buffering
- Slide (capsule resize, friction, camera lower)
- Crouch (62% height, 50% speed, ceiling-aware)
- Wall-run (gravity, max-up-speed cap, 0.8s cooldown)
- Grapple hook (ground + air, 55u range, rope visual, hit marker)
- Jetpack hover (hold Space while airborne after release, 5s energy)
- Double air jump

Tooling:
- IMGUI debug overlay (Tab toggle controls, sensitivity []/], jetpack bar, crosshair)
- 3 tunable movement presets (Balanced / Snappy / Aggressive)
- Traversal trial timer + checkpoint system
- Editor bootstrap (Tools > Dimension Breach > Bootstrap)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add TURN server support for WAN relay fallback

- Change WebRTCClient.init to accept [RTCIceServer] (was [String])
- Add Metered.ca Open Relay TURN servers to defaults (free 20GB/month)
  - turn:openrelay.metered.ca:80 (UDP/TCP)
  - turn:openrelay.metered.ca:443 (TCP)
  - turns:openrelay.metered.ca:443 (TLS)
- Set iceTransportPolicy = .all to allow both direct and relay paths
- Keep Google STUN for NAT discovery alongside TURN relay

This fixes WAN connections that fail with STUN-only when both peers
are behind symmetric NAT (the connection stays stuck in 'connecting').

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add configurable hostname and admin user password (#187)

* feat(bootstrap): add configurable hostname and admin user password

- Add `hostname` and `admin_user_password` to config.toml template
- Fix /etc/hosts to properly map 127.0.0.1→localhost and 127.0.1.1→hostname
- Set admin user password via password_hash('sha512') for Cockpit login
- Require `hostname` in bootstrap playbook config validation

* docs: update documentation for hostname and admin_user_password config

Update bootstrap, config, and getting-started docs to reflect new
config.toml settings. Clarify admin_user_password is optional (for
Cockpit login) while hostname is required.

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails (#189)

* fix(fail2ban): use systemd backend for SSH jails instead of missing auth.log

* docs(fail2ban): document jail configuration and systemd backend usage

* docs update

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(ansible_user): decouple admin user creation from password setting

Admin user creation was gated on admin_user_password being set, which
skipped user creation entirely when password was empty. Split into two
tasks: always create user, conditionally set password. Also use
update_password: on_create for idempotent reruns.

* fix(bootstrap): tighten /etc/hosts regex to avoid prefix matching

^127\.0\.0\.1 also matches 127.0.0.10. Add \s after the IP to ensure
only the exact address followed by whitespace is matched.

* test(ansible_runner): assert hostname in bootstrap required keys

* docs: clarify admin_user_password is optional across all docs

Mark admin_user_password as optional (only needed for Cockpit login)
in first-deployment, bootstrap, secrets, and cockpit documentation.

* docs: clarify optional password and fallback in layers and cockpit docs

* fix(bootstrap): add hostname validation before VPS connection

* fix(ansible_user): add no_log to password task to prevent secret leakage

* chore: sync config.example.toml with TEMPLATE in user_config.rs

Adds missing keys: hostname, admin_user_password, bichon_*,
headscale_subdomain, paperless_subdomain, paperless_tailscale_ip,
restic_*, tailscale_login_server, and vdirsyncer_*.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(cli): include hardening.yml in tag resolution

The dependency resolver only scanned infrastructure.yml and apps.yml,
making `auberge ansible run --tags fail2ban` unresolvable.

Add hardening.yml to target_playbooks and PLAYBOOK_ORDER, and add role
names (fail2ban, kernel_hardening) as tags for consistency with other
playbooks.

Closes #191

* refactor(cli): address review feedback on tag resolution

- Remove duplicate target_playbooks array, reuse PLAYBOOK_ORDER as
  single source of truth
- Rename misleading test to test_resolve_fail2ban_tag_targets_hardening

Define Phase 2 roadmap: Polish & Depth (closes #73)

Replace completed Phase 1 roadmap (8 items) with 4 new Phase 2 items:
1. Visual Polish & Game Feel (animations, particles, transitions)
2. Tutorial & Onboarding System (first-run guidance, contextual hints)
3. Garden Expansion & Structures (larger grids, greenhouse, compost, rain barrel)
4. Achievements & Cosmetic Rewards (badges, seed packet skins, gallery)

  CopilotStudioSamples/CustomExternalUI with TypeScript, mock Direct Line,
  5 theme presets, adaptive cards gallery, and CLI test runner
- Port Power CAT Kit patterns (test automation, webchat playground,
  adaptive cards gallery) to run locally without Power Platform
- Add docs/copilot-products.md cheat sheet distinguishing Copilot Studio
  vs M365 Copilot vs GitHub Copilot
- Fix docs/DECISION_GUIDE.md to separate Copilot Studio from Power Platform
- Enhance topics.json with conversation nodes, fallback, and escalation
- Fix copilot-plugin.json misattribution (Teams compose ext, not Copilot Studio)

Attribution: Power CAT Copilot Studio Kit (MIT), CopilotStudioSamples (MIT)

Add mission statement system and clean up dead code

- Create .peel/mission.md defining Peel's core purpose and agent guidelines
- Add MissionService.swift for loading/caching mission statements per repo
- Add MissionToolsHandler.swift with mission.get and mission.check MCP tools
- Wire mission into agent chain prompts via buildEnrichedPrompt()
- Register mission tools in MCPServerService (property, init, delegate, dispatch)

Dead code cleanup:
- Remove iOS platform declarations from 6 local packages (macOS-only app)
- Remove UIKit import and #if canImport(UIKit) from PeelUI Color+Extensions
- Remove unused Combine import from ContentView.swift
- Remove 3 deprecated swarm MCP tools (stun-test, p2p-logs, request-logs)
  including definitions, handlers, and case dispatch
